channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🏴 Russia launches unannounced nuclear exercise, including Belarusian launch sites | Defense News
item.description: The dual messaging − nuclear muscle-flexing at home, high-profile diplomacy abroad − fits a pattern the Kremlin has used repeatedly.

item.title: 🌍 The Doublespeak of Lebanon’s Other Shiite Leader | Foreign Policy
item.description: Hezbollah’s fate will be decided by the Amal Movement’s calculations about its own future.

item.title: 🏴 NATO eastern deterrence strategy takes shape around ‘autonomous zone’ | Defense News
item.description: “What we see in Ukraine, no vehicle movements are in the gray zone at all,” one Latvian commander said. “Any movement is destroyed.”

item.title: 🏴 Italy settles on Airbus tanker purchase in swing toward European equipment | Defense News
item.description: The deal marks a shift from Boeing to Airbus for the Italian Air Force, which flies four Boeing 767-based tanker aircraft which entered service from 2011.

item.title: 🌏 The Navy Needs Precise Mass and Here Is How to Get There | War on the Rocks
item.description: Maintaining deterrence in the Indo-Pacific and around the world requires the U.S. Navy to change what it builds and how it fights. Sen. Roger Wicker observed in 2024 that the United States’ approach to fleet design and ship construction is “too small and too old.” The current…

item.title: 🔐 Machine Overmatch: What Salt Typhoon Reveals About China’s Data-Centric Intelligence Strategy | War on the Rocks
item.description: What if the next decisive intelligence advantage isn’t a recruited insider but a nation’s ability to model entire societies from its digital exhaust? Salt Typhoon’s multi-year cyber campaigns against U.S. telecommunications networks and critical infrastructure demonstrate…

item.title: 🌏 More risk and cost: de-scoped Collins LOTE strengthens case for looking to Japan | ASPI Strategist
item.description: The reduced Collins-class submarine Life-of-Type Extension (LOTE) program, announced on 19 May, will cost more than previously expected and deliver less capability. The government’s decision to cut back the scope of the work also increases ...

item.title: 🌏 Why Were Shots Fired in the Philippines Senate? | Foreign Policy
item.description: The gunfight is the latest twist in a power struggle between two dynasties.

item.title: 🌏 Iran as Vietnam, Ukraine as Korea | Foreign Affairs
item.description: Similar wars end in similar ways.

item.title: 📍 Why Mexico’s Cartels Are So Hard to Defeat | Foreign Affairs
item.description: The real test of Sheinbaum’s security strategy.

item.title: 📍 Northern Australia needs an elite First Nations crisis capability program | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Each wet season, Northern Australia and the wider Oceania region are reminded that climate stress is no longer episodic. Cyclones, floods, extreme heat, infrastructure failure and displacement arrive with uncomfortable frequency. And they are becoming ...

item.title: 🌍 ‘Everybody is going underground’: CENTCOM head calls for new tech to hit buried targets | Defense One
item.description: Adm. Brad Cooper was praised, and grilled, by lawmakers during his first HASC hearing.

item.title: ⚛️ AC-130J Gunship With Mini Cruise Missiles Paired With AESA Radar To Undergo Tests | The War Zone
item.description: The plan is to demonstrate a fully integrated, longer-range strike capability that could keep the AC-130 relevant in high-end conflicts. The post AC-130J Gunship With Mini Cruise Missiles Paired With AESA Radar To Undergo Tests appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 ‘Numbers are getting worse’: The head of the IMF on the Iran war shock, inflation, AI risks, and more | Atlantic Council
item.description: Watch IMF Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva talk with Atlantic Council President and CEO Frederick Kempe from the Europe Gulf Forum in Greece. The post ‘Numbers are getting worse’: The head of the IMF on the Iran war shock, inflation, AI risks, and more appeared first on…

item.title: 🏛️ Navy greenlights low-rate production of drone refueler | Defense One
item.description: A contract for three MQ-25A aircraft is expected this summer.

item.title: 🌏 Why China Is Cracking Down on Elite Education | Foreign Policy
item.description: The campaign is motivated by corruption concerns and an old-fashioned egalitarianism.

item.title: 🏛️ One year in, Army’s transformation efforts are under fire | Defense One
item.description: Hegseth is rethinking his order to offload old systems and bring in new tech.

item.title: 🏴 US plans to shrink forces available to NATO during crises, sources say | Defense News
item.description: The Trump administration is planning to shrink the pool of U.S. military capabilities available to assist NATO's European nations in a major crisis.

item.title: 🏴 Dispatch from Bucharest: NATO’s center of gravity is shifting eastward | Atlantic Council
item.description: A recent summit shows that Eastern and Northern European allies are increasingly shaping the Alliance’s strategic agenda on defense and resilience. The post Dispatch from Bucharest: NATO’s center of gravity is shifting eastward appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🌏 China is bolstering food security with biomanufacturing. Australia should, too | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Australia urgently needs sovereign biomanufacturing capability to reduce its exposure to fragile global supply chains, especially those tied to agri-food systems. A national biomanufacturing strategy is essential to achieve this. China and the United States ...

item.title: 🏴 From Moscow to Crimea, Ukraine is winning the drone war against Russia | Atlantic Council
item.description: As Russia’s full-scale invasion enters a fifth summer, it is becoming increasingly apparent that the tide in the drone war has turned in Ukraine’s favor, writes David Kirichenko. The post From Moscow to Crimea, Ukraine is winning the drone war against Russia appeared first on…

item.title: 🏴 Russia’s New Two-Seat Su-57 Felon Takes Its First Flight | The War Zone
item.description: Official imagery confirms that the two-seat Su-57D version of the Russian fighter has begun flight testing. The post Russia’s New Two-Seat Su-57 Felon Takes Its First Flight appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: ⚛️ Space Force names Sandhoo as head of new missile warning/tracking PAE | Breaking Defense
item.description: As of May 11, Sandhoo is double hatted as both head of the PAE and director of the Space Development Agency.

item.title: 🛡️ NORTHCOM standing up ‘Nordic Bridge’ to boost US coordination in Arctic | Breaking Defense
item.description: Gen. Guillot said, “The goal is always to defend as far away from the homeland as we can” and that special operations forces will play a key role.

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine’s experience can teach Europe how to defend against Russia | Atlantic Council
item.description: Ukraine's wartime transformation should inform European defense modernization in both technology and doctrine. Europe must now choose: Seize the opportunity to adapt or procrastinate and risk future defeat, write Valeriya Ionan and Nicolas Dunais. The post Ukraine’s experience…

item.title: 🔐 Hackers using AI just found a ‘zero-day.’ The spyware industry is watching. | Atlantic Council
item.description: Spyware companies that hack into devices for the purpose of surveillance and data extraction are primed to exploit this new opportunity. The post Hackers using AI just found a ‘zero-day.’ The spyware industry is watching. appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏛️ Air Force grounds T-38 fleet after Mississippi mishap | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Air Force said an “operational pause” is necessary as an investigation into a May 12 crash of the aircraft proceeds. Inspections to return aircraft to flight are expected to begin as soon as this week.

item.title: 🏛️ Navy, Marine Corps back longer amphib readiness cycles, request more ships | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Navy currently utilizes a 36-month Optimized Fleet Response Plan (OFRP), but leaders want to stretch it to 56 months.

item.title: 🕵️ Demining the Strait of Hormuz: Q&A with Scott Savitz | RAND
item.description: Scott Savitz is a senior engineer at RAND, a specialist in naval operations and technologies, and an expert in mine warfare. In this interview he discusses the threat of naval mines and the challenge of demining the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 🏴 What Does Putin Want From Xi? | Foreign Policy
item.description: Russia seeks to resolve several trade issues during this week’s summit. But China holds the cards.
